_meta.unit: A2.8 Davos / Sam residual (the LAST A-roundup unit)
_meta.note: 27th major-arc enrichment dip; CLOSES the A-roundup. TWO halves. HALF A = DAVOS (WIRE+ENRICH, heavy dedup -- the S93 Wyman/Davos deception spine + S138 Blackwater cluster + S151 GNC hub all pre-built): lit the causally-islanded deception cluster (staged-execution ENABLES the NEW davos-sent-to-fetch-rickon-from-skagos hub; execution-of-lookalike ENABLES frey-witnesses-attest ENABLES grand-northern-conspiracy -- the deception-unlocks-the-conspiracy trunk), built the ONLY Davos gap (the Skagos commission hub, SUB_BEAT_OF the GNC, wyman COMMANDS_IN / davos AGENT_IN), wired Davos's ACOK->ADWD arc spine (Blackwater MOTIVATES davos via sons-to-fire grief; rescue->Dragonstone->arrest->Hand->White Harbor->Wolf's Den spatial+info spine), the Sisters detour (godric INFORMS davos Tywin-dead), wex-pyke WITNESS_IN bran-s-party-splits-from-rickon (the chalk-revealed Rickon-escape, cross-arc Davos<->Bran-party seam), wyman's hidden whodunit (DECEIVES jared by_lie + OPPOSES roose). DEDUP: khorane RESCUES davos + davos SEEKS rickon + wyman SEEKS rickon + wyman REVEALS_TO davos + the 4 deception SUB_BEAT_OF beats + wyman CONSPIRES_WITH stannis all EXIST -- NOT re-minted. HALF B = SAM (BUILD -- the ASOS Other-kill + Coldhands rescue were dyad-only, the AFFC Citadel road was 0% built): minted sam-kills-the-other [north] (the dragonglass Other-kill, first Other slain by a man in centuries; sam AGENT_IN + dragonglass WIELDED_IN + fight-at-the-fist ENABLES it), coldhands-rescues-sam-and-gilly [north] (DISTINCT from bran-meets-coldhands [ADWD]; coldhands AGENT_IN + RESCUES sam+gilly), death-of-maester-aemon [] (at sea off Dorne; aemon VICTIM_IN, sam OFFICIATES+MOURNS, gilly MOURNS, aemon REVEALS_TO sam the Daenerys-belief disclosure); wired Sam into his own Fist hub (PARTICIPATES_IN + WITNESS_IN + jeor COMMANDS_IN), the Citadel road (TRAVELS_TO braavos/oldtown/citadel + REVEALS_TO alleras [the full Wall-intelligence dump]), Dareon brawl (ATTACKS); the MARQUEE cross-half seam leeching-of-edric-storm MOTIVATES death-of-maester-aemon (the Edric-blood precedent is WHY Jon ships Aemon south to his death -- wires the Davos half's Edric beat into the Sam half's Aemon death). DROPPED at synthesis: C-02/C-03 (staged-exec/frey-pies PART_OF GNC -- already SUB_BEAT_OF, dup), C-08 (mutiny ENABLES jon-election -- over-distal, Bowen-Marsh interim castellan), C-09 (bran-meets-coldhands ENABLES coldhands-rescues -- TEMPORALLY BACKWARDS, the Sam rescue [ASOS] predates Bran's meeting [ADWD]), rickon LOCATED_AT skagos (Skagos un-named in davos-04, inferential -- davos SEEKS rickon + hub prose cover it), the Marwyn/Alleras intelligence-chain extension + marwyn-sails-to-daenerys node + xhondo node (deferred to a future Citadel/Marwyn dip -- scope + theory-adjacency). Fist-dup battle-of-the-fist-of-the-first-men ALREADY same_as fight-at-the-fist (redirect stub exists, no action). Theory-gated CLEAN: Aemon's TPTWP belief recorded as a disclosure act (REVEALS_TO), never an asserted prophecy edge. EXCLUDES TWOW. The 9 verify:true edges (causal MOTIVATES/ENABLES + interpretive OPPOSES/ALLIES_WITH) carry pending for the independent fresh-verify. New hubs ship containers via their node bodies. After this dip the A-roundup is CLOSED -> next = granular-dip PLANNING session (Matt S130).

edges.quote: Dragonstone loomed larger with every stroke of the oars.
edges.note: Davos's post-rescue return to Dragonstone. The existing khorane RESCUES davos covers the rescue; this names the destination of the voyage.

edges.quote: The cell was warmer than any cell had a right to be.
edges.note: After his return, Davos is arrested by Axell Florent and held in Dragonstone's dungeon. The arrest event (ser-axell-florent-arrests-davos) exists; this is the spatial confinement edge.

edges.quote: Captain Khorane had told him of the end of Stannis's hopes, on the night the river burned.
edges.note: Khorane is the source of the Blackwater-aftermath intelligence Davos receives before reaching Dragonstone. Distinct from the existing RESCUES dyad.

edges.quote: she gave my sons to the fire. I will cut the living heart from her breast
edges.note: Davos's four eldest sons died in the wildfire at the Blackwater; the loss drives his revenge-resolve against Melisandre, which restarts his Dragonstone arc (the return that gets him arrested). Gives the heavily-built battle-of-the-blackwater hub an outgoing MOTIVATES edge. Distinct from the existing leeching-of-edric-storm MOTIVATES davos (the Edric-rescue motive).

edges.quote: I make it folly . . . aye, and cowardice.
edges.note: Davos openly opposes Axell's proposed Claw Isle raid in the king's presence, despite Axell's bridge threats. No OPPOSES edge on file.

edges.quote: He smoothed the letter flat upon his knee and began to read by the light of the magic sword.
edges.note: Davos reads the Night's Watch's plea for help aloud to Stannis -- the disclosure that turns the king north. The pivot of the Stannis-goes-north arc.

edges.quote: I am teaching the art to your son, at the king's command. Let me teach you as well.
edges.note: Maester Pylos teaches Davos to read on Dragonstone -- the skill that lets him read the Wall's letter to Stannis (D6). Enriches Davos's Dragonstone arc.

edges.quote: Rise again, Davos Seaworth, and rise as Lord of the Rainwood, Admiral of the Narrow Sea, and Hand of the King.
edges.note: Stannis raises Davos from prisoner to Hand of the King in the Chamber of the Painted Table. The arc's turning point; clean dyad (no hub needed).

edges.quote: This is Sisterton, on Sweetsister.
edges.note: Storm-blown to Sisterton (Godric Borrell's seat) on the way to White Harbor. No spatial edge on file.

edges.quote: You are telling me that Tywin Lannister is dead?
edges.note: Godric Borrell breaks the news of Tywin Lannister's death (and Salladhor's abandonment) to Davos at Breakwater. Davos's incredulous reply frames Godric as the teller.

edges.quote: The Merry Midwife stole into White Harbor on the evening tide
edges.note: Davos arrives incognito at White Harbor as Stannis's envoy. No spatial edge on file.

edges.quote: Even in the gloom of the Wolf's Den, Davos Seaworth could sense that something was awry this morning.
edges.note: After the staged public arrest, Davos is held in the Wolf's Den. Distinct from the Dragonstone imprisonment (D2).

edges.quote: He stayed up in the tree a long time.
edges.note: Wex Pyke (mute) hid in a tree at Winterfell and witnessed the survivors leaving and splitting up -- the intelligence (relayed by chalk to Glover/Wyman) that reveals where Rickon went. Cross-arc seam wiring the Davos/Manderly cluster to the Bran-party split event.

edges.quote: When treating with liars, even an honest man must lie.
edges.note: Wyman openly names his deception of the Freys (Jared is his primary Frey contact in the Merman's Court). The whodunit underlay of the Grand Northern Conspiracy.

edges.quote: It was the Bastard of Bolton who put Winterfell to the sword
edges.note: Privately Wyman reveals his enmity toward the Boltons even as he publicly feigns loyalty to Roose, his Warden of the North. The hidden opposition that powers the GNC.

edges.quote: I will take Stannis Baratheon as my king.
edges.note: The commission scene seals Manderly's allegiance to Stannis, mediated through Davos. Distinct from the existing wyman CONSPIRES_WITH stannis (the conspiracy dyad); this is the Davos<->Wyman personal compact.

edges.quote: Smuggle me back my liege lord, and I will take Stannis Baratheon as my king.
edges.note: Wyman is the orderer of the Skagos commission -- his price for White Harbor's allegiance. Orderer role on the new hub.

edges.quote: For this I must have a man who's sailed in darker waters and knows how to slip past dangers, unseen and unmolested.
edges.note: Davos is the designated operative for the Skagos mission (the smuggler Wyman needs). Agent role on the new hub.

edges.quote: Smuggle me back my liege lord, and I will take Stannis Baratheon as my king.
edges.note: The Skagos commission is a beat inside the Grand Northern Conspiracy -- matching the existing wyman-stages-fake-execution + manderly-bakes-frey-pies SUB_BEAT_OF grand-northern-conspiracy structure.

edges.quote: It would not do for you to be seen, my lord. You are supposed to be dead.
edges.note: The staged death IS the cover story that makes the covert Skagos mission possible -- Davos can travel unseen only as a 'dead man'. ENABLES (precondition created by the deception). Lights the causally-islanded deception cluster forward.

edges.quote: Lord Davos, you will not know, but you are dead.
edges.note: The displayed head and hands of the lookalike are the physical evidence the Frey witnesses see and later attest to at the small council. Sub-beat to sub-beat causation within the deception cluster (both nodes pre-exist).

edges.quote: I did not dare defy King's Landing so long as my last living son remained a captive.
edges.note: The attestation that 'Davos is dead' helps free Wylis Manderly from Lannister custody -- removing the sole constraint (the hostage) that kept Wyman from acting on the GNC. The deception unlocks the conspiracy. Cross-chapter (the release is AFFC Cersei); quote is Wyman's own statement of the constraint.

edges.quote: They had been fifty when they fled the Fist, maybe more, but some had wandered off in the snow
edges.note: Sam was present throughout the Fist disaster and its retreat -- his POV narrates it -- yet no edge wired him to the hub he narrates.

edges.quote: He remembered the dead coming over the stones with arrows in their faces and through their throats
edges.note: Sam directly saw the wights breach the ringwall -- text SHOWS the perception. Distinct from PARTICIPATES_IN (presence/survival).

edges.quote: the Lord Commander roared that night on the Fist
edges.note: Mormont commanded the defense and the breakout at the Fist (ordering fire arrows). The hub had only wiki-sourced edges; this adds the command role.

edges.quote: the Other's armor was running down its legs in rivulets as pale blue blood hissed and steamed around the black dragonglass dagger in its throat
edges.note: Sam stabs the Other with the dragonglass dagger -- the first Other slain by a man in centuries. Agent role on the new hub.

edges.quote: the black dragonglass dagger in its throat
edges.note: The dragonglass dagger (from the Fist cache, given by Jon) is the instrument of the kill -- pays off the dragonglass Chekhov's gun. Artifact-in-event role.

edges.quote: They had been fifty when they fled the Fist
edges.note: The rout at the Fist scattered the survivors and left Sam straggling, which is how the Other came upon him. ENABLES (the disaster created the isolated-straggler conditions for the encounter).

edges.quote: they made Sam and Grenn tell the Lord Commander
edges.note: Sam reports to Mormont that dragonglass slew the Other -- the intelligence that reframes the Watch's true enemy and weapon. Mormont takes Sam's dragonglass in response.

edges.quote: Beneath the trees, a man muffled head to heels in mottled blacks and greys sat astride an elk.
edges.note: Coldhands (the elk-rider) commands the raven-swarm that drives off the wights besieging Sam & Gilly. Agent role on the new hub.

edges.quote: The shout cut through the night, through the shrieks of a thousand ravens.
edges.note: Coldhands rescues Sam from the wights at the abandoned village. DISTINCT from bran-meets-coldhands (ADWD) -- this ASOS rescue predates Bran's meeting.

edges.quote: reaching down with a gloved hand to pull Gilly up behind him
edges.note: Gilly (with her newborn) is pulled up onto the elk first -- both she and Sam are rescued. Separate RESCUES edge.

edges.quote: He was Aemon Targaryen. And now his watch is ended.
edges.note: Maester Aemon dies at sea aboard the Cinnamon Wind. The patient role on his death-event hub.

edges.quote: Sam donned his blacks to say the words
edges.note: With no septon aboard, Sam performs the funeral rite and gives Aemon's eulogy. OFFICIATES role on the hub.

edges.quote: Sam hung his head and began to weep, his sobs so loud and wrenching that they made his whole body shake.
edges.note: Sam's overt grief, which shapes his entire Oldtown mission.

edges.quote: There were tears in her eyes as well.
edges.note: Gilly also mourns Aemon at the funeral.

edges.quote: You must tell them, Sam,
edges.note: Aemon's dying charge -- carry the warnings to the Citadel's archmaesters -- drives the rest of Sam's mission. The death MOTIVATES Sam's Citadel errand.

edges.quote: If the red woman requires king's blood for her spells
edges.note: MARQUEE cross-half seam. Jon cites the king's-blood-for-magic danger (the precedent the Edric-Storm leeching established) as why Aemon -- a Targaryen -- must leave the Wall; that journey kills him. Wires the Davos-half Edric beat into the Sam-half Aemon death.

edges.quote: Daenerys is the one, born amidst salt and smoke. The dragons prove it.
edges.note: Aemon's deathbed disclosure to Sam of his belief that Daenerys (not Rhaegar) is the prince that was promised. Recorded as a disclosure act (what Aemon told Sam) -- NOT an asserted prophecy edge; theory-gated.

edges.quote: This ship will take us as far as Braavos
edges.note: First leg of the Citadel road (Eastwatch -> Braavos aboard Blackbird).

edges.quote: They reached Oldtown on a cold damp morning
edges.note: Sam reaches Oldtown aboard the Cinnamon Wind -- destination of the Citadel road.

edges.quote: The gates of the Citadel were flanked by a pair of towering green sphinxes
edges.note: Sam arrives at the Citadel gates -- the arc terminus.

edges.quote: all the rest came spilling out; the wights at the Fist of First Men
edges.note: Sam pours out the whole Wall intelligence -- Others, dragonglass, Coldhands, Aemon's dying words -- to Alleras the Sphinx. The first Watch intelligence to reach the Citadel in centuries; the Citadel-road payoff.

edges.quote: He punched the singer in the face and in the belly, then began to pummel him about the shoulders with both hands.
edges.note: Sam attacks the deserter Dareon in a Braavos brawl. Dareon's death (killing-of-dareon) + dareon BETRAYS nights-watch already exist; this is Sam's overt confrontation.
